name: SkyWater Technology, Inc
business: SkyWater is a U.S.-based independent pure-play semiconductor foundry providing foundational-node manufacturing, advanced technology development, and advanced packaging services through an integrated multi-site operating model. The company operates exclusively in the United States with facilities in Minnesota, Texas, and Florida, serving customers that require secure domestic manufacturing, long product life cycles, high reliability, and close engineering collaboration. Its business model integrates production-scale manufacturing with advanced technology development, enabling customers to transition specialized semiconductor technologies from development to volume production.
namedSuppliers: Rohm and Haas EM LLC (subsidiary of DuPont)

exposures.sentence: SkyWater Technology, Inc., together with its consolidated subsidiaries, is a U.S.-based, independent, pure-play semiconductor foundry providing foundational-node manufacturing, advanced technology development, and advanced packaging services through an integrated, multi-site operating model.

Our primary end markets include: Aerospace and defense (A&D): Including trusted and secure microelectronics, radiation-tolerant and radiation-hardened devices, and mission-critical systems supporting U.S. government and defense programs.

Quantum computing represents a growing segment of SkyWater’s business and reflects increasing demand for specialized, domestic manufacturing capabilities to support next-generation computing platforms.
Bio-health: Including medical devices, diagnostics, and imaging systems.
Automotive: Particularly applications requiring reliability, longevity, and stable supply over extended product lifetimes.
Industrial: Including sensing, control, and power management applications.

a $12.9 million year-over-year growth in the Company’s advanced compute technology market due to the addition of new customers and acceleration of existing programs as companies continue to invest in the emerging technology.

A breach of our security systems or a cyberattack that disrupts our operations or results in the breach of confidential information about us, our technology, or our customers could harm our business and reputation, and could expose us to costly regulatory enforcement and other liability.
We may use artificial intelligence in our business and operations, and challenges with properly managing its use could harm our business and expose us to costly liability.

catalysts.sentence: On January 25, 2026, the Company entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger (the Merger Agreement) with IonQ, Inc.
the transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including regulatory and stockholder approvals, and, if approved, is expected to close in the second or third quarter of 2026.
On June 30, 2025, the Company completed the acquisition of all of the issued and outstanding membership interests of Spansion Fab 25, LLC (Fab 25)

catalysts.event: Multi-year supply agreement with Infineon (take-or-pay) effective
catalysts.sentence: the Company entered into a multi-year supply agreement with certain of Infineon’s subsidiaries under a take-or-pay arrangement for the four-year period following the closing of the Transaction

catalysts.event: Amended Loan Agreement with Siena (Revolver) effective
catalysts.sentence: On June 30, 2025, we entered into an Amended Loan Agreement with Siena and the other lenders party thereto
In June 2025, the Company entered into an agreement to sell and leaseback a furnace over a 36 month period.

catalysts.event: Signed preliminary memorandum of terms for CHIPS Act funding
catalysts.sentence: In December 2024, we signed a preliminary memorandum of terms that provides for up to $16 million in funding pursuant to the CHIPS and Science Act, which will be combined with $19 million in incentives from the State of Minnesota’s Forward Fund.
